req.title: Message to provider (boundary)
req.statement: A patient shall send a message to their provider; messages containing attachments shall scan for disallowed PHI exfiltration patterns. Boundary cases on timestamps and numeric elements must be tested.

doc.reasoning: R-HLT-050 covers three distinct risk areas: (1) happy-path message delivery with provider notification, (2) PHI-exfiltration blocking on attachments, and (3) boundary behaviour on timestamps and numeric fields. I apply EP for the happy path (valid partition), NEG for the disallowed attachment (error pathway), BVA for timestamp/character-count boundaries, STC for the state change from draft→sent→notified, and DT for the attachment-scan decision matrix. Each test is self-contained via fixtures that create and teardown their own data, making them idempotent.

tests.name: test_patient_sends_plain_text_message_successfully
preconditions: Provider exists in the system with a known provider_id
No prior unread messages exist for the provider in this session
actions: POST /messages with valid subject, body text (no attachment), and provider_id
GET /notifications?provider_id=<id> to verify provider notification created
expected: HTTP 201 with message_id in response body
Stored message body matches submitted body
Provider notification record exists with status='unread'
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_patient_sends_plain_text_message_successfully(auth_session, provider_id, api_base_url, cleanup_messages):
    payload = {
        "provider_id": provider_id,
        "subject": "Test appointment query",
        "body": "Please confirm my appointment for next Monday."
    }
    resp = auth_session.post(f"{api_base_url}/messages", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201, got {resp.status_code}"

    msg_id = resp.json()["message_id"]
    cleanup_messages.append(msg_id)

    stored = auth_session.get(f"{api_base_url}/messages/{msg_id}")
    assert stored.status_code == 200
    assert stored.json()["body"] == payload["body"]

    notif_resp = auth_session.get(
        f"{api_base_url}/notifications",
        params={"provider_id": provider_id, "message_id": msg_id}
    )
    assert notif_resp.status_code == 200
    notifications = notif_resp.json().get("items", [])
    assert any(n["status"] == "unread" for n in notifications), "Provider notification not found"

tests.name: test_message_with_phi_attachment_is_blocked
preconditions: A file containing disallowed PHI patterns (SSN, DOB) is available on the test host
actions: POST /messages with a multipart body including an attachment that contains SSN pattern '\d{3}-\d{2}-\d{4}'
Confirm response error code references PHI scan failure
Verify no message record was persisted via GET /messages
expected: No message_id created; GET /messages returns no new entry
trace: R-HLT-050: error pathway – blocked when disallowed attachment detected
tests.executable:
import pytest
import io

def test_message_with_phi_attachment_is_blocked(auth_session, provider_id, api_base_url):
    phi_content = b"Patient SSN: 123-45-6789, DOB: 01/01/1980"
    files = {"attachment": ("record.txt", io.BytesIO(phi_content), "text/plain")}
    data = {"provider_id": provider_id, "subject": "Records", "body": "See attached."}

    resp = auth_session.post(f"{api_base_url}/messages", data=data, files=files)
    assert resp.status_code in (400, 422), f"Expected 4xx, got {resp.status_code}"

    error_body = resp.json()
    assert error_body.get("error_code") == "PHI_EXFILTRATION_BLOCKED", \
        f"Unexpected error_code: {error_body.get('error_code')}"

    list_resp = auth_session.get(f"{api_base_url}/messages", params={"provider_id": provider_id})
    messages = list_resp.json().get("items", [])
    blocked_msgs = [m for m in messages if m.get("subject") == "Records"]
    assert len(blocked_msgs) == 0, "Blocked message must not be persisted"

tests.name: test_message_timestamp_at_epoch_zero_boundary
preconditions: API accepts an optional client_timestamp field in the message payload
System validates timestamps must be >= Unix epoch (0)
actions: Assert response indicates acceptance or a defined validation error
POST /messages with client_timestamp=-1 (below epoch lower bound)
Assert HTTP 400 with validation error for negative timestamp
expected: client_timestamp=0 returns HTTP 201 or documented 422 with 'TIMESTAMP_TOO_OLD'
No message persisted for negative timestamp

tests.name: test_message_body_at_maximum_character_boundary
preconditions: System enforces a maximum message body length of 5000 characters
actions: POST /messages with body of exactly 5000 characters
trace: R-HLT-050: boundary cases on numeric elements
tests.executable:
import pytest

MAX_BODY_LEN = 5000

def test_message_body_at_maximum_character_boundary(auth_session, provider_id, api_base_url, cleanup_messages):
    base = {"provider_id": provider_id, "subject": "BVA length"}

    # Exactly at max: should succeed
    payload_max = {**base, "body": "A" * MAX_BODY_LEN}
    resp_max = auth_session.post(f"{api_base_url}/messages", json=payload_max)
    assert resp_max.status_code == 201, \
        f"Max-length body rejected unexpectedly: {resp_max.status_code}"
    cleanup_messages.append(resp_max.json()["message_id"])

    # One over max: should fail
    payload_over = {**base, "body": "A" * (MAX_BODY_LEN + 1)}
    resp_over = auth_session.post(f"{api_base_url}/messages", json=payload_over)
    assert resp_over.status_code == 400, \
        f"Over-max body not rejected: {resp_over.status_code}"
    assert resp_over.json().get("error_code") == "BODY_TOO_LONG"

tests.name: test_message_state_transitions_draft_to_sent_to_notified
preconditions: Messages API supports draft creation and explicit send action
actions: POST /messages/draft to create a message in DRAFT state
GET /notifications for provider and assert notification status='unread' (NOTIFIED state)
expected: Send action transitions message to status='sent'
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_message_state_transitions_draft_to_sent_to_notified(auth_session, provider_id, api_base_url, cleanup_messages):
    draft_payload = {"provider_id": provider_id, "subject": "STC draft", "body": "Will send soon."}
    draft_resp = auth_session.post(f"{api_base_url}/messages/draft", json=draft_payload)
    assert draft_resp.status_code == 201
    draft = draft_resp.json()
    assert draft["status"] == "draft"
    draft_id = draft["message_id"]
    cleanup_messages.append(draft_id)

    send_resp = auth_session.post(f"{api_base_url}/messages/{draft_id}/send")
    assert send_resp.status_code == 200
    assert send_resp.json()["status"] == "sent"

    notif_resp = auth_session.get(
        f"{api_base_url}/notifications",
        params={"provider_id": provider_id, "message_id": draft_id}
    )
    assert notif_resp.status_code == 200
    items = notif_resp.json().get("items", [])
    assert any(n["status"] == "unread" for n in items), "Expected unread notification after send"

tests.name: test_attachment_scan_decision_table_allowed_vs_disallowed_types
preconditions: Scan service distinguishes allowed (image/png) from disallowed (text/plain with PHI patterns)
actions: POST /messages with an image/png attachment containing no PHI patterns
POST /messages with a text/plain attachment containing an SSN pattern
trace: R-HLT-050: attachments shall scan for disallowed PHI exfiltration patterns
R-HLT-050: decision table – type × PHI presence
tests.executable:
import pytest
import io

def test_attachment_scan_decision_table_allowed_vs_disallowed_types(auth_session, provider_id, api_base_url, cleanup_messages, minimal_png_bytes):
    base = {"provider_id": provider_id, "subject": "DT scan", "body": "Scan matrix test."}

    # Row 1: image/png, no PHI -> allowed
    files_ok = {"attachment": ("img.png", io.BytesIO(minimal_png_bytes), "image/png")}
    r1 = auth_session.post(f"{api_base_url}/messages", data=base, files=files_ok)
    assert r1.status_code == 201, f"PNG no-PHI should be accepted: {r1.status_code}"
    cleanup_messages.append(r1.json()["message_id"])

    # Row 2: text/plain, SSN present -> blocked
    phi = b"SSN: 987-65-4321"
    files_phi = {"attachment": ("note.txt", io.BytesIO(phi), "text/plain")}
    r2 = auth_session.post(f"{api_base_url}/messages", data=base, files=files_phi)
    assert r2.status_code in (400, 422), f"PHI txt should be blocked: {r2.status_code}"
    assert r2.json().get("error_code") == "PHI_EXFILTRATION_BLOCKED"

    # Row 3: no attachment -> allowed
    r3 = auth_session.post(f"{api_base_url}/messages", json=base)
    assert r3.status_code == 201, f"No-attachment should be accepted: {r3.status_code}"
    cleanup_messages.append(r3.json()["message_id"])
